title: Duncan Gallie (ed.): Economic Crisis, Quality of Work, and Social Integration: The European Experience
abstract: This volume edited by Duncan Gallie provides rich evidence of how the economic crisis of 2008–2009 and its aftermath shaped the quality of work and social integration in Europe. Bringing together the expertise of several renowned scholars of labour market sociology, Gallie’s book breaks new ground by offering the first in-depth, truly comparative study of whether the labour environment and social structure of Europe changed during this recession, the most severe of its kind since World War II. With its focus on immediate post-recession Europe, however, this book neglects recent developments and longer-term consequences. Instead, the authors provide a detailed account of the immediate responses in various fields such as employment, quality of work (skill change, opportunities for skill development, job control, work intensity, work stress, job insecurity, and work-family conflict), and social integration (employment commitment, subjective well-being, and political legitimacy). Investigating the impact in such a multidimensional setting allows for the detection of common trends and potential trade-offs, which is one of the biggest strengths of this volume.
